introduction: Inflammation of the iris and ciliary body. Usually occurs without any associated systemic inflammation and may be recurrent.

features: Pain may feature due to ciliary spasms.
Keratic precipitates on the corneal endothelium
Miosis due to spasm of sphincter pupillae

investigations: Chest radiograph to exclude sarcoidosis and tuberculosis
Spinal radiograph (especially lumbrosacral segment) to exclude ankylosing spondilytis
Serology for rheumatoid factor, antinuclear antibody, etc

drug: Corticosteroid drops: Betamethasone sodium phosphate 0.1% (1 - 2 hourly until inflammation is controlled, then reduce frequency)
Subconjunctival injection of steroid if severe
Atropine sulphate 0.5% or 1%; 1 drop up to 4 times daily
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Avoid atropine drops if there is risk of acute glaucoma
